description_long: This object is a heavy-duty electric jackhammer, commonly used in urban construction and roadwork. It features a robust rectangular main body with two horizontal handles for grip and control, and a prominent trigger or switch on one side. The lower section houses a cylindrical mechanism that connects to a long, pointed chisel bit designed for breaking concrete or asphalt. The overall build is solid, with visible seams and mechanical joints, and the tool appears to be constructed primarily from metal with some plastic or rubberized components for grip. There are no visible brand markings or logos, and the surface is a uniform, matte gray color. The design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and durability for demanding environments.
description: A heavy-duty electric jackhammer with a rectangular body, dual handles, and a pointed chisel bit, finished in matte gray. Built for urban construction and demolition tasks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual horizontal handles, main body, and the upper section of the chisel bit.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile, the length of the chisel bit, and the cylindrical lower mechanism.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with handles and main body visible, but no controls or switches apparent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the side profile and the pointed chisel bit.
